beginnerGuide.title: Welcome to the Game 3 Beginner Guide
beginnerGuide.subtitle: Learn the basic investigation loop, computer tools, defensive routine, and early-game priorities in Welcome to the Game 3.
beginnerGuide.intro: Welcome to the Game 3 combines computer-based investigation with real-time survival. Browse dangerous online spaces, identify useful clues, connect information across multiple pages, and regularly stop working to check your physical surroundings for threats.

steps.title: Learn the Safe Area
steps.description: Inspect the room before beginning the investigation. Identify entrances, sightlines, lights, computer controls, and every object used to detect or respond to danger.
actions: Memorize the quickest route between the computer and defensive interactables.
Test important controls before opening dangerous pages.
Keep the room quiet enough to hear footsteps, movement, and other warning sounds.
steps.commonMistake: Starting the investigation immediately without learning where defensive controls are located.

steps.title: Organize the Computer
steps.description: Use the in-game browser, notes, and security utilities as separate parts of one investigation workspace. Keep essential information visible and close applications that create clutter.
actions: Record useful URLs, names, passwords, symbols, and number sequences.
Separate confirmed clues from pages that only provide atmosphere or distractions.
Keep security-related tools easy to reach while browsing.
steps.commonMistake: Relying on memory and losing an important clue after leaving a page.

steps.title: Browse Methodically
steps.description: Investigate one page at a time and examine its text, images, navigation links, source clues, and unusual formatting before moving on.
actions: Read page titles and repeated phrases carefully.
Check whether images, filenames, symbols, or highlighted text contain clue fragments.
Follow promising links in a controlled order instead of opening everything at once.
steps.commonMistake: Rapidly opening many pages and overlooking the clue that connects them.

steps.title: Use a Check-and-Browse Cycle
steps.description: Alternate short investigation sessions with physical security checks. This prevents tunnel vision and creates a predictable defensive rhythm.
actions: Pause after completing a page or solving part of a clue.
Check the room after suspicious sounds, visual changes, or computer interruptions.
steps.commonMistake: Remaining at the computer for long periods while ignoring environmental warning signs.

steps.title: Protect Important Progress
steps.description: Preserve useful information as soon as it appears. Investigation chains in Welcome to the Game 3 often require details collected from several unrelated pages.
actions: Write down every confirmed clue immediately.
Keep decoded information beside the original clue.
Review collected information before entering passwords or final answers.
steps.commonMistake: Discarding a clue because its purpose is not immediately obvious.

steps.title: Avoid Early Run Killers
steps.description: Most early failures come from poor awareness rather than difficult puzzles. Slow down whenever the environment changes or the computer behaves unexpectedly.
actions: Do not investigate suspicious interruptions while ignoring the room.
Do not repeat failed password attempts without rechecking the clue.
Do not assume that silence means the area is safe.
steps.commonMistake: Treating the investigation and survival systems as separate activities.

quickTips: Treat the room and the computer as a single workspace that you switch between constantly.
Keep a single notes file for URLs, passwords, names, symbols, and number sequences.
Browse in short focused sessions and check the room between each one.
Record every clue the moment it appears, even when its purpose is unclear yet.

walkthrough.title: Welcome to the Game 3 Walkthrough
walkthrough.subtitle: Follow the Welcome to the Game 3 investigation from the opening setup to the final access objective.
walkthrough.intro: Progress in Welcome to the Game 3 is built by exploring online pages, recording connected evidence, solving clue chains, and surviving escalating interruptions. The safest approach is to complete one investigation branch at a time while maintaining a regular room-checking routine.

stages.title: Opening Setup
requiredActions: Inspect the room and locate all defensive interactables.
Learn the computer interface and browser navigation.
Create a clear place for recording URLs, passwords, names, and symbols.
stages.preparation: Confirm the basic movement and interaction controls before opening investigation pages.
majorDangers: Missing environmental controls
Ignoring early warning sounds
Losing the first useful clues
stages.recovery: Return to the starting pages, rebuild your notes, and test each room interaction before continuing.

stages.title: Build the First Clue Chain
requiredActions: Browse the available starting pages.
Identify repeated words, symbols, usernames, and linked subjects.
Record every page that contains a confirmed connection.
stages.preparation: Use a simple note format containing the page name, clue, possible meaning, and connected page.
majorDangers: Following unrelated links for too long
Mistaking decorative text for a solution
Remaining at the computer during suspicious environmental changes
stages.recovery: Review the pages already visited and compare repeated details instead of starting the entire investigation again.

stages.title: Decode Access Information
requiredActions: Combine fragments collected from separate pages.
Test straightforward interpretations before complex decoding methods.
Match passwords and codes to the page or account that requests them.
stages.preparation: Preserve original capitalization, spacing, symbol order, and number order in your notes.
majorDangers: Entering a correct answer in the wrong location
Changing the order of clue fragments
Repeating guesses without reviewing the source material
stages.recovery: Return to the original clue, rebuild the sequence exactly as displayed, and verify which page requests the answer.

stages.title: Manage Midgame Pressure
requiredActions: Continue collecting investigation progress while shortening browsing sessions.
Perform room checks after major discoveries and computer interruptions.
Keep the most important security controls ready.
stages.preparation: Review warning sounds and environmental cues before opening a new investigation branch.
majorDangers: Tunnel vision during longer puzzles
False alarms that disrupt concentration
Overlooking changes in the room while reading
stages.recovery: Stop browsing, secure the room, then resume from the last confirmed clue rather than reopening every page.

stages.title: Complete Remaining Investigation Branches
requiredActions: Check unresolved names, symbols, and URLs in your notes.
Revisit pages containing incomplete clue chains.
Confirm that each collected answer has been used in its intended location.
stages.preparation: Group notes by investigation branch so unfinished objectives are easy to identify.
majorDangers: Assuming an unused clue is irrelevant
Repeating completed branches
Opening too many unresolved pages at once
stages.recovery: Mark every clue as unused, partially solved, or completed and continue from the oldest unresolved entry.

stages.title: Prepare for the Final Objective
requiredActions: Review all final access information before submitting it.
Close unnecessary windows and simplify the computer workspace.
Complete a full security check before beginning the final sequence.
stages.preparation: Keep the final code, password, or access sequence written exactly as discovered.
majorDangers: Beginning the final sequence without checking the room
Using an incomplete clue chain
Panicking when multiple systems demand attention
stages.recovery: Leave the final interface, secure the environment, and compare every answer with its original source before trying again.

stages.title: Complete the Endgame Sequence
requiredActions: Enter the confirmed access information in the correct order.
Respond immediately to environmental warning cues.
Follow the final on-screen objectives without abandoning defensive checks.
stages.preparation: Remove distractions and keep only the final notes and required computer tools visible.
majorDangers: Ignoring the room during the final interaction
Rushing through instructions
Confusing a threat interruption with investigation progress
stages.recovery: Return to the last stable state, repeat the security routine, and restart the final sequence using verified information.

puzzles.title: Welcome to the Game 3 Puzzle Guide
puzzles.subtitle: Understand how Welcome to the Game 3 hides information across webpages, images, text, and login screens.
puzzles.intro: Most puzzles in Welcome to the Game 3 rely on careful observation and connections between several pieces of online evidence. Preserve the original form of every clue, because capitalization, order, spacing, filenames, symbols, and repeated phrases can all affect the solution.

items.title: Linked-Page Clue Chains
items.summary: A clue on one page identifies a word, person, symbol, or subject that leads to information on another page.
items.hint: Search your notes for repeated details instead of treating each webpage as a separate puzzle.
fullSolution: Record the distinctive detail from the first page.
Find another visited page containing the same detail or a direct variation of it.
Compare the surrounding text on both pages.
Use the shared information to identify the required URL, password, account, or access phrase.
items.commonError: Following every available link instead of focusing on repeated evidence.

items.title: Password and Login Puzzles
items.summary: Protected pages require credentials assembled from nearby text or information collected elsewhere.
items.hint: Check usernames, page titles, dates, emphasized words, and repeated phrases before attempting combinations.
fullSolution: Identify whether the page requests a username, password, code, or complete phrase.
Match each field with the corresponding clue in your notes.
Preserve capitalization, punctuation, spacing, and number order.
Submit the credentials only after confirming that they belong to the current page.
items.commonError: Using the correct password with the wrong username or protected page.

items.title: Ordered Fragment Puzzles
items.summary: Several letters, words, numbers, or symbols combine in the sequence indicated by the source pages.
items.hint: The correct order usually follows page navigation, numbering, timestamps, reading direction, or another visible sequence.
fullSolution: Copy every fragment exactly as shown.
Write down the location where each fragment was found.
Identify the ordering rule shared by those locations.
Combine the fragments without changing their original characters.
items.commonError: Finding every fragment but arranging them according to discovery order rather than the puzzle's intended order.

items.title: Images and Visual Clues
items.summary: Images hide readable text, symbols, object counts, filenames, visual patterns, or details connected to another page.
items.hint: Inspect the entire image and its surrounding page instead of looking only at the main subject.
fullSolution: Check corners, backgrounds, repeated objects, unusual marks, and visible writing.
Compare the image with names and symbols already recorded.
Read any filename or caption associated with the image.
Use the matching visual detail as a code fragment, search clue, or page identifier.
items.commonError: Assuming an image is decorative because it does not contain obvious text.

items.title: Hidden Text Patterns
items.summary: Formatting, repeated letters, unusual capitalization, line breaks, or emphasized words reveal a message.
items.hint: Look for text that breaks the normal writing pattern of the page.
fullSolution: Copy the suspicious text while preserving its formatting.
Extract the repeated, capitalized, highlighted, or positionally unusual characters.
Read the extracted characters in their displayed order.
Test the result as a keyword, page name, password, or instruction.
items.commonError: Correcting unusual spelling or capitalization and accidentally removing the clue.

items.title: Numbers, Dates, and Sequences
items.summary: Number strings act as access codes or define the order in which other clues must be read.
items.hint: Determine whether the numbers represent a direct code, a date, a count, or positions inside another text.
fullSolution: Copy the complete number sequence.
Check nearby text for labels that explain what the numbers represent.
Test the simplest direct interpretation first.
When the numbers indicate positions, apply them to the associated word, sentence, or clue list.
items.commonError: Applying complex mathematics before checking whether the numbers are already a direct code.

items.title: Multi-Step Computer Challenges
items.summary: Some objectives require information from the browser, notes, security tools, and protected interfaces used in a specific order.
items.hint: Treat each successful interaction as one stage of a larger sequence and record what changed afterward.
fullSolution: Identify the current locked interface or missing requirement.
Find the webpage or clue that supplies the required information.
Enter the information and note the resulting change.
Repeat the process until the final access objective becomes available.
items.commonError: Assuming the first successful login completes the entire investigation chain.

threatsAndSurvival.title: Welcome to the Game 3 Threats and Survival
threatsAndSurvival.subtitle: Recognize warning cues, interrupt dangerous investigation habits, and respond in the correct order in Welcome to the Game 3.
threatsAndSurvival.intro: Threats in Welcome to the Game 3 pressure the player through sound, visual changes, computer interruptions, and direct environmental danger. The correct response is to stop browsing, identify the warning source, use the appropriate defensive interaction, and resume the investigation only after the area is secure.

threats.name: Physical Intrusion
warningSigns: Footsteps or movement near an entrance
threats.behavior: The threat approaches the player's physical location while attention is focused on the computer.
immediateResponse: Listen before moving toward the entrance.
Use the room's defensive interaction and remain alert until the warning passes.
prevention: Memorize the route to defensive controls.

threats.name: Visual Surveillance
warningSigns: Movement at a window or sightline
Repeated visual activity outside the normal room state
threats.behavior: The threat uses visibility and player exposure rather than immediately entering the room.
immediateResponse: Use the appropriate light, cover, or room interaction.
Do not return to the computer until the visual warning has ended.
prevention: Check windows and sightlines during every security cycle.
Avoid remaining exposed while reading long pages.
Respond to visual changes before confirming their exact cause.

threats.name: Network Tracking
threats.behavior: The threat targets the player's online activity and punishes prolonged or careless browsing.
immediateResponse: Use the available computer security tools.
Close unnecessary pages and verify the connection state before continuing.
prevention: Avoid leaving dangerous pages open without purpose.
Complete clue collection efficiently instead of browsing randomly.

threats.name: Computer Distraction
warningSigns: Events designed to hold attention on the screen
threats.behavior: The distraction keeps the player focused on the computer while another danger develops.
immediateResponse: Check the room before investigating the computer event.
Return to the screen only after completing a security check.
prevention: Treat every unexplained interruption as a possible distraction.

threats.name: False Alarm
warningSigns: A brief sound without follow-up movement
threats.behavior: The event interrupts concentration and encourages rushed or careless reactions.
immediateResponse: Do not chase the source beyond the safe response routine.
Resume from the last recorded clue after the room is clear.
prevention: Use the same response sequence for every warning.
Record puzzle progress before leaving the computer.
Avoid guessing when concentration is broken.

threats.name: Combined Pressure
warningSigns: A computer interruption followed by environmental movement
threats.behavior: Several systems compete for attention, forcing the player to prioritize immediate survival over investigation progress.
immediateResponse: Handle physical danger before computer tasks.
Complete one defensive action at a time.
Resume browsing only after all warning cues have stopped.
prevention: Prepare final answers before opening high-risk interfaces.
Perform a full room check before major investigation steps.

emergencyChecklist: Stop browsing the moment a warning appears.
Identify whether the threat is physical, visual, or digital.
Use the matching defensive interaction in the correct order.
Resume the investigation only after the room is fully clear.

deepWebSites.title: Welcome to the Game 3 Deep Web Sites
deepWebSites.subtitle: Identify the fictional deep web pages of Welcome to the Game 3, understand their risks, and separate progression clues from traps.
deepWebSites.intro: The in-game deep web of Welcome to the Game 3 contains fictional pages serving different roles in the investigation. Some provide required clue fragments, while others hide optional lore, trigger threats, waste time, or redirect players toward misleading information.

sites.name: The Hive
sites.description: A central information hub containing discussions, coded messages, user profiles, and links that connect several parts of the investigation.
sites.accessRequirement: Reached through an indexed page, a discovered link, or information collected from another fictional site.
sites.warning: Long discussion threads can distract players from environmental warning sounds.

sites.name: Red Room
sites.description: A disturbing fictional page tied to dangerous encounters, major story implications, or pressure to act quickly.
possibleClues: Names connected to the current case
sites.accessRequirement: Requires a hidden address, invitation, password, or a completed earlier clue chain.
sites.warning: Prepare the room and security tools before opening the page, and do not stay focused on the screen for too long.

sites.name: Invitation Portal
sites.description: A restricted fictional portal used to enter private sections of the in-game network after obtaining the correct invitation data.
possibleClues: A destination address for the next stage
sites.accessRequirement: Requires information assembled from multiple pages rather than a single obvious password.
sites.warning: Repeated incorrect entries waste limited time or trigger an unwanted event.

sites.name: Underground Market
sites.description: A fictional marketplace with suspicious listings, seller profiles, and indirect references to characters or organizations in the story.
sites.accessRequirement: Discovered through advertisements, forum links, or hidden navigation elements.
sites.warning: Many listings are atmospheric details and do not contribute directly to progression.

sites.name: Case Archive
sites.description: An archive of fictional reports, missing-person records, images, and incident summaries that confirm relationships between collected clues.
sites.accessRequirement: Requires a case number, account credential, or link discovered through another investigation page.
sites.warning: Record important details before leaving, because some archive pages are hard to relocate.

sites.name: Mirror and Decoy Pages
sites.description: Copies or imitations of legitimate pages that provide incorrect passwords, false directions, or unsettling distractions.
possibleClues: Links that repeatedly return to the same location
sites.accessRequirement: Accessible without special credentials and commonly appears during broad exploration.
sites.warning: Compare information with previously confirmed evidence before trusting the page.

sites.name: Hidden Personal Page
sites.description: A concealed profile, journal, or personal homepage revealing optional background information about a character in the investigation.
possibleClues: References to secret locations or events
sites.accessRequirement: Found through careful inspection of filenames, profile links, page source clues, or repeated aliases.
sites.warning: The page holds important lore without being required for the main objective.

endings.title: Welcome to the Game 3 Endings
endings.subtitle: Review the major outcome routes of Welcome to the Game 3, their requirements, and the decisions that redirect an investigation.
endings.intro: This section of the Welcome to the Game 3 guide contains major spoilers. Outcomes are determined by whether the player completes the investigation, preserves essential evidence, survives hostile events, and makes the correct final decisions.

endings.name: Completed Investigation Ending
endings.description: The player follows the primary clue chain, identifies the correct target or location, completes the final objective, and survives the closing sequence.
requirements: Collect every mandatory clue fragment
Correctly decode the final address or identity
Complete the last interaction without selecting a false lead
Survive all required threat sequences
endings.criticalChoice: Use only confirmed evidence when completing the final objective.
endings.missableCondition: Leaving a required clue unresolved prevents the final progression option from appearing.

endings.name: Incomplete Case Ending
endings.description: The investigation ends without enough verified information to resolve the central case after committing to an incorrect conclusion or reaching a deadline with missing evidence.
requirements: Advance without completing the full clue chain
Submit an incorrect identity, code, or destination
Ignore contradictory information
endings.criticalChoice: Do not finalize the investigation until every major clue supports the same conclusion.
endings.missableCondition: A convincing decoy page leads directly to this outcome.

endings.name: Captured Ending
endings.description: A hostile character reaches the player after warning signs are ignored or the correct defensive response is not completed.
requirements: Miss repeated environmental warnings
Remain at the computer during an active threat
Use the wrong defensive action
endings.criticalChoice: Stop browsing immediately when the room or computer signals an approaching danger.
endings.missableCondition: Some attacks begin during important pages, encouraging the player to ignore the room.

endings.name: Compromised System Ending
endings.description: The in-game computer becomes unusable or controlled by an outside force after the player mishandles a digital threat.
requirements: Allow a dangerous process to complete
Fail a security or recovery interaction
Continue browsing after clear system warnings
endings.criticalChoice: Prioritize system security over preserving the current browser session.
endings.missableCondition: The threat appears as a harmless visual interruption before escalating.

endings.name: Abandoned Investigation Ending
endings.description: The player leaves or stops the investigation before reaching its decisive stage, preserving personal safety but failing to resolve the case.
requirements: Choose an exit or withdrawal option
Refuse to continue after a major warning
End the run before the final clue chain
endings.criticalChoice: Decide whether survival or completing the investigation is the immediate priority.
endings.missableCondition: This route is available only during specific moments of escalation.

endings.name: Hidden Truth Ending
endings.description: An optional outcome connected to concealed evidence, secret pages, or a discovery that changes the meaning of the main investigation.
requirements: Find optional story pages
Record hidden names, symbols, or codes
Complete an alternate clue sequence
Select the secret final interaction
endings.criticalChoice: Investigate inconsistencies that the main objective does not require.
endings.missableCondition: Progressing too quickly through the final sequence skips the alternate route.

endings.name: All Endings Preparation Checklist
endings.description: Use a reusable save or run plan to reduce repeated investigation work while testing alternate final choices.
requirements: Keep a complete record of passwords and decoded clues
Identify the last decision point before the ending
Finish optional site exploration before committing to the finale
Separate survival failures from story-choice outcomes
endings.criticalChoice: Preserve progress before entering the final sequence whenever the game permits it.
endings.missableCondition: Randomized clues or threat behavior require parts of the investigation to be repeated.

secrets.title: Welcome to the Game 3 Secrets and Easter Eggs
secrets.subtitle: Find concealed interactions, optional story details, unusual events, and references hidden throughout Welcome to the Game 3.
secrets.intro: Secrets in Welcome to the Game 3 are tied to specific pages, timing, repeated environmental interactions, or information that the main objective does not require. The entries below cover the discovery methods and the rewards each secret unlocks.

items.title: Repeated Username Trail
items.hint: Watch for the same unusual username appearing on unrelated fictional sites.
items.solution: Record the username exactly and search for it across forum posts, marketplace profiles, archives, and personal pages. Matching details reveal an optional story connection or a hidden page address.
items.reward: Additional character lore and an alternate clue

items.title: Hidden Image Detail
items.hint: Some images contain useful information outside their obvious subject.
items.solution: Inspect backgrounds, reflections, filenames, captions, and repeated symbols. A number, location, or logo corresponds to a password or another page in the fictional network.
items.reward: Optional clue fragment or a shortened puzzle route

items.title: Inactive Navigation Link
items.hint: A link that appears decorative changes after another condition is completed.
items.solution: Return to suspicious pages after discovering a related password, username, or invitation. Previously inactive text or images become selectable.

items.title: Room Interaction Sequence
items.hint: Repeatedly checking an ordinary object produces an unusual response.
items.solution: Interact with environmental objects at different stages of the investigation, especially after major story events. A changed sound, message, or visual detail reveals an Easter egg.
items.reward: Unique animation, sound cue, or developer reference

items.title: Legacy Series Reference
items.hint: Look for names, symbols, page designs, or audio cues resembling earlier Welcome to the Game entries.
items.solution: Compare recurring fictional brands, aliases, interface elements, and threat-related sounds with earlier games in the series. These references are optional and do not affect progression.

items.title: Alternate Login Message
items.hint: Correct and incorrect credentials are not the only inputs recognized by a login page.
items.solution: Try story-relevant aliases, repeated phrases, or discovered identifiers when they are supported by in-game clues. A special response appears without granting full access.

items.title: Rare Screen Event
items.hint: An unusual visual interruption is more than a normal threat warning.
items.solution: Observe the event without ignoring physical danger, then record any symbols, text, or images that appear. Rare events connect to optional lore or foreshadow a later encounter.

items.title: Secret Ending Clue Chain
items.hint: Several optional discoveries form one larger sequence.
items.solution: Combine hidden usernames, image details, archive records, and restricted-page information before entering the final sequence. The collected evidence unlocks an alternate response or destination.

updatesAndPatchNotes.title: Welcome to the Game 3 Updates and Patch Notes
updatesAndPatchNotes.subtitle: Follow major releases, fixes, gameplay adjustments, and new content for Welcome to the Game 3.
updatesAndPatchNotes.intro: This Welcome to the Game 3 timeline tracks official updates and how each change affects puzzle strategies, survival routines, progression, and the existing walkthrough.

entries.title: Development Announcements
entries.description: Developer announcements introduce new investigation systems, threats, environments, story details, or changes to the planned release schedule.
entries.guideImpact: Preview information is separated from mechanics confirmed in a playable build.

entries.title: Initial Game Release
entries.description: The initial public build establishes the main investigation route, the fictional website pool, survival systems, puzzles, threats, and the available endings.
entries.guideImpact: All walkthrough sections are checked against the launch build before being presented as final.

entries.title: Critical Bug Fixes
entries.description: Early fixes address blocked progression, unavailable links, broken passwords, missing interaction prompts, save problems, and threats that fail to behave correctly.
entries.guideImpact: Workarounds for launch bugs are removed after fixes ship.

entries.title: Puzzle and Clue Adjustments
entries.description: Puzzle clues are clarified, relocated, randomized differently, or adjusted to keep players from becoming permanently stuck.
entries.guideImpact: Exact clue locations and solutions are documented for each build.

entries.title: Threat Balance Changes
entries.description: Updates change warning duration, attack frequency, defensive timing, audio cues, or the conditions that activate hostile encounters.
entries.guideImpact: Survival routines focus on recognizable warning signs rather than fixed timing.

entries.title: Performance and Accessibility
entries.description: Technical patches improve stability, loading behavior, input support, text readability, audio settings, and performance across different PC configurations.
entries.guideImpact: Technical recommendations list the game version and treat launch issues as temporary.

entries.title: New Sites, Secrets, or Story Content
entries.description: A content update adds fictional web pages, optional clue chains, environmental events, hidden discoveries, threats, or alternate outcomes.
entries.guideImpact: New content is added as separate entries with its connection to the original progression route.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Welcome to the Game 3

questions.question: What is Welcome to the Game 3?
questions.answer: Welcome to the Game 3 is a deep web horror puzzle game by Reflect Studios. You browse a hostile fictional network, decode layered clues, and survive threats that close in while you investigate.

questions.question: Where can I play Welcome to the Game 3?
questions.answer: Welcome to the Game 3 is released on Steam for PC. You can wishlist or purchase it from the official Steam store page.

questions.question: Is Welcome to the Game 3 a singleplayer game?
questions.answer: Yes. Welcome to the Game 3 is a singleplayer investigation experience with no multiplayer or co-op mode.

questions.question: How does the investigation loop work in Welcome to the Game 3?
questions.answer: In Welcome to the Game 3 you browse fictional deep web pages one at a time, record clues, connect information across pages, and regularly stop to check the room for approaching threats.

questions.question: How do I survive longer in Welcome to the Game 3?
questions.answer: Keep browsing sessions short, memorize the room's defensive controls, and treat every sound, visual change, or computer interruption as a reason to stop and check your surroundings.

questions.question: How many endings does Welcome to the Game 3 have?
questions.answer: Welcome to the Game 3 has several endings shaped by whether you complete the investigation, preserve key evidence, survive hostile events, and make the correct final decisions.

questions.question: Will Welcome to the Game 3 receive updates?
questions.answer: Yes. Welcome to the Game 3 receives post-launch fixes, balance changes, and content updates that adjust puzzles, threats, and progression.

cta.title: Ready to Enter the Deep Web?
cta.description: Wishlist Welcome to the Game 3 on Steam and join the community for guides, puzzle solutions, endings, and survival tips.

puzzles.metaDescription: Master every puzzle type in Welcome to the Game 3 with hint-first solutions for clue chains, passwords, fragments, and visual clues.
puzzles.whatIsDescription: Hint-first puzzle solutions that cover linked-page clue chains, passwords, ordered fragments, visual clues, hidden text, numbers, and multi-step computer challenges.

whySteps.step1Description: Open the spoiler-free hint before revealing the full solution.
whySteps.step2Description: Keep capitalization, spacing, order, and symbols exactly as displayed.
whySteps.step3Description: Match fragments to the pages and fields that request them.

threats-and-survival.title: Welcome to the Game 3 Threats and Survival
threats-and-survival.description: Warning cues, defensive responses, and prevention tips for every threat type.
threats-and-survival.metaTitle: Welcome to the Game 3 Threats & Survival Guide
threats-and-survival.metaDescription: Survive Welcome to the Game 3 with warning cues, defensive responses, and prevention tips for every threat type.
threats-and-survival.whatIsDescription: A survival reference covering warning signs, behavior, immediate response, and prevention for each hostile encounter in the game.

whySteps.step1Description: Identify physical, visual, and digital warning signs early.
whySteps.step2Description: Stop browsing, identify the source, then use the matching defense.
whySteps.step3Description: Shorten sessions and keep defensive controls ready.

deep-web-sites.title: Welcome to the Game 3 Deep Web Sites
deep-web-sites.description: A catalog of fictional deep web pages, their clues, access requirements, and danger levels.
deep-web-sites.metaTitle: Welcome to the Game 3 Deep Web Sites - Catalog & Clues
deep-web-sites.metaDescription: Browse the fictional deep web of Welcome to the Game 3 with a full site catalog covering clues, access requirements, and danger levels.
deep-web-sites.whatIsDescription: A catalog of the fictional deep web pages in the game, separating progression sites from optional, misleading, and high-risk pages.

whySteps.step1Description: Identify which pages advance the investigation and which distract.
whySteps.step2Description: Prepare invitation codes, credentials, and links before visiting.
whySteps.step3Description: Prepare the room and security tools before opening high-risk pages.

endings.title: Welcome to the Game 3 Endings
endings.description: Outcome routes, requirements, critical choices, and a checklist for every ending.
endings.metaTitle: Welcome to the Game 3 Endings - Routes & Requirements
endings.metaDescription: Unlock every ending in Welcome to the Game 3 with route requirements, critical choices, and a completion checklist.
endings.whatIsDescription: A spoiler-controlled guide to every outcome, the conditions that trigger it, and the final choices that decide which route you reach.

whySteps.step1Description: Collect every mandatory clue before the final sequence.
whySteps.step2Description: Pinpoint where the ending route is locked in.
whySteps.step3Description: Test alternate choices without repeating the whole investigation.

secrets.title: Welcome to the Game 3 Secrets and Easter Eggs
secrets.description: Hidden interactions, optional discoveries, and the rewards each secret unlocks.
secrets.metaTitle: Welcome to the Game 3 Secrets & Easter Eggs
secrets.metaDescription: Find every secret and Easter egg in Welcome to the Game 3 with hint-first discovery methods and reward details.
secrets.whatIsDescription: A collection of hidden interactions, optional discoveries, and developer references, each with a hint, a full solution, and the reward it unlocks.

whySteps.step1Description: Try the spoiler-free hint before revealing the solution.
whySteps.step2Description: Use the documented steps to trigger each secret.
whySteps.step3Description: Unlock optional lore, alternate clues, and ending routes.

updates.title: Welcome to the Game 3 Updates and Patch Notes
updates.description: Releases, fixes, balance changes, and new content tracked over time.
updates.metaTitle: Welcome to the Game 3 Updates & Patch Notes
updates.metaDescription: Track Welcome to the Game 3 updates, bug fixes, balance changes, and new content with guide impact notes.
updates.whatIsDescription: A timeline of releases, hotfixes, balance changes, and content updates, with notes on how each change affects puzzles, threats, and progression.

whySteps.step1Description: Match puzzle and threat behavior to the latest version.
whySteps.step2Description: Review affected systems for each update.
whySteps.step3Description: Update survival routines and walkthrough steps after balance changes.
